source.reference: Generic Job Description Team Assistant (S1 LS1).pdf

description_original.content:
Team Assistant

Organisation: ICMPD
Location: Brussels, Belgium
Engagement: Fixed-term
Compensation: Monthly net-base salary: €2,662.00
Deadline: 27 July 2026
Work arrangement: On-site
Source: https://careers.icmpd.org/Home/JobOpeningDetails?jobOpeningId=1187
Source documents: Generic Job Description Team Assistant (S1 LS1).pdf

## Overview

This is a joint recruitment covering two Team Assistant positions across two ICMPD Global Initiatives: the European Union Migration and Displacement Capacity Assistance Project (EU-MIDCAP) and the Return and Reintegration Facility (RRF). Each Team Assistant will be assigned to one of the two initiatives.

## Role

The Team Assistant is responsible for providing smooth, efficient, and reliable administrative support to managers and project staff for project-related activities. Reporting to the respective Project Manager, they work closely with project team members and provide guidance to new staff and personnel on ICMPD’s regulatory framework, standard administrative practices, and operating procedures.

## Language requirements

- Required: Proficiency in spoken and written English
- Assets: Proficiency in French and Spanish

## Project assignments

### EU-MIDCAP

The European Union Migration and Displacement Capacity Assistance Project (EU-MIDCAP) is an EU-funded, DG INTPA-supported, consortium-led peer-to-peer facility. It supports partner-country authorities, civil society organisations, and regional bodies in strengthening migration and forced-displacement management systems and cooperation with the EU and its Member States.

The Team Assistant will provide cross-cutting support to the project and several Technical Assistance Actions, including in French- and Spanish-speaking countries, and will report to one of the Project Managers.

### Return and Reintegration Facility (RRF)

The Return and Reintegration Facility (RRF) is an EU-funded programme supported by DG HOME. Established in 2022, it helps EU Member States strengthen the effectiveness, coordination, and sustainability of their return and reintegration systems. More information is available at https://www.reintegrationfacility.eu/.

The Team Assistant will join the RRF Facility Support and Knowledge Team, providing horizontal administrative support across the RRF’s four sub-teams, comprising around 25 team members in total, and will report to one of the Project Managers.

## Generic profile (S1/LS1) summary

The Team Assistant provides administrative support including correspondence, reports, databases, expert recruitment/procurement support, travel organisation, contract monitoring, and events/meetings logistics. Requires bachelor's degree or equivalent (or high-school plus two additional years), at least two years relevant experience, events experience, strong English; SAP an asset. Classified S1/LS1.
